human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for production-specific boundary discipline, talent-service artifact fidelity, child-performer and visa escalation controls, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to screen-production talent-service intake, call-time and travel readiness, deal-memo and guild-document awareness, child-performer prerequisite handling, and evidence-backed routing tied to production, talent, venue, and rights records.

Authority sources cover SAG-AFTRA production and work-rule guidance, California labor guidance for minors, studio teachers, child performer services, and talent-agency licensing, AMPTP work-visa workflow guidance, and CSATF minors safety guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported minor and rights exceptions, business-affairs handoff, repeat travel failures, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/broadcast-film-and-streaming-production-services/talent-services-specialist/ so talent services stays separate from casting, payroll, immigration, legal, security, and child-performer compliance authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around minors, rights changes, payroll or deal-term negotiations, visa-sensitive work, and schedule-driven exception pressure.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated talent services specialist that supports evidence-backed performer service handling and policy-aware escalation for broadcast, film, and streaming productions.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
